businessSummary.oneLiner: Leader in government and commercial AI data analytics platforms, operating on three pillars: Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI Platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+56%), with US commercial revenue surpassing $500M, demonstrating successful expansion into the private sector. Recently, Keel joined the US Navy ShipOS project, extending defense AI applications to submarine production. While the stock surged 1,860% over 3 years, it experienced 10+ drawdowns of 20% or more during that period, making it a high-volatility name.

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Over 10x the S&P 500 average of 22x. Extremely elevated vs software average of 35x

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS $1.34. Significant growth premium but still demanding

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $4.48B, FY2026E $7.41B (+65.5%). Growth is actually accelerating

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top tier among SaaS companies. Achieving high profitability and growth simultaneously

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: FCF $2.1B. Best-in-class cash generation in the software industry

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Backlog
keyMetrics.interpretation: Per Motley Fool. Revenue visibility secured for years ahead

growthDrivers.title: NYT: 'Silicon Valley Bet on War. It's Being Rewarded'
growthDrivers.description: NYT highlighted Palantir as a prime example of defense AI investment success. Defense tech going mainstream. Major media acknowledging defense AI profitability positively impacts investor sentiment.

growthDrivers.title: US Navy ShipOS $448M: AI Applied to Submarine Production
growthDrivers.description: Keel joined Palantir on the US Navy ShipOS project. AI applied to submarine production lines, expanding defense AI scope from battlefield to military manufacturing.

growthDrivers.title: Wedbush $230 + UBS $200: Wall Street Target Price Upgrade Rush
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush maintains $230 Buy, UBS raises target from $180 to $200. Representing +48% and +31% upside respectively. 16 out of 28 analysts rate Buy.

growthDrivers.title: $11.2B Revenue Backlog + $10B Army Contract
growthDrivers.description: Per Simply Wall St, a $10B Army contract is reshaping the defense software market. $11.2B backlog secures revenue visibility for years.

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Revenue Outlook $7.41B (+65.5%), Growth Accelerating
growthDrivers.description: Growth rate accelerating from 56% (FY2025 $4.48B) to 65.5% (FY2026E $7.41B). AI platform demand surging.

growthDrivers.title: NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS Reference Architecture
growthDrivers.description: Palantir Sovereign AI OS included in NVIDIA hardware reference architecture. Potential to set the standard for government AI infrastructure worldwide.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of track record in government and defense AI. Security clearance requirements create barriers to entry. ShipOS expands AI application to military manufacturing.

moats.description: Top-level security clearance with US DoD and intelligence agencies. Competitors cannot enter.
AI deployed in submarine production and naval shipyards. Expanding beyond battlefield to all military manufacturing.
Sovereign AI OS + NVIDIA hardware ecosystem. Setting government AI infrastructure standards globally.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform. General-purpose approach vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data lakehouse. Expanding into analytics platform territory

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI. Trails Palantir in scale and track record

recentNews.title: NYT: Silicon Valley Bet on War, Being Rewarded - Palantir as Key Example
UK Privacy Backlash Raises Risk for NHS Contract
Yahoo Finance: 1,860% Gain in 3 Years, 10 Drawdowns of 20%+ - Volatility Analysis
UBS Raises Target $180->$200, Wedbush Maintains $230 Buy
Keel Joins Palantir, US Navy ShipOS Project ($448M)

risks.title: Extreme Valuation + Fed Hawkish Environment
risks.description: P/E of 221x with Fed delaying rate cuts. The worst combination for growth stocks. 10+ drawdowns of 20% over 3 years. Iran tensions and inflation warnings drove Dow -400, intensifying growth stock pressure.

risks.title: Persistent Insider Selling
risks.description: CEO Alex Karp selling approximately $6M in shares daily. Management actively taking profits at current price levels.

risks.title: Government Revenue Concentration + UK NHS Privacy Controversy
risks.description: Privacy backlash in the UK raising risk for NHS contract. High government revenue dependence makes it vulnerable to political risk.

risks.title: Share Dilution
risks.description: Stock-based compensation (SBC) causing approximately 5% annual dilution. Per-share value dilution eroding long-term returns.

risks.title: Intensifying AI Market Competition
risks.description: Big tech (Microsoft, AWS, Google) investing heavily in AI platforms. Competition heating up in the enterprise AI market.

buyReasons: NYT highlights defense AI success + Wedbush $230, UBS $200 simultaneous upgrades
ShipOS $448M: AI expanding to military manufacturing, maximizing defense AI scope
$11.2B backlog + $10B Army contract + 65.5% growth, best-in-class revenue visibility
3-year 1,860% return: track record of recovering from every 20%+ drawdown

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush $230 (+48%), UBS $200 (+31%) simultaneous upgrades. NYT highlights defense AI success. 16 out of 28 analysts rate Buy.

overallOpinion: NYT highlighted Palantir as the prime example of 'Silicon Valley betting on war' in defense AI. Wedbush $230 and UBS $200 simultaneous upgrades show Wall Street joining the bull thesis.
However, UK privacy backlash is creating risk for the NHS contract, and Fed hawkish comments sent the Dow -400, pressuring growth stocks broadly. A P/E of 221x clashes with the rate environment.

Technically, trading below the 50-day MA ($158) with RSI at 46 (neutral). The 200-day MA ($127) remains below, keeping the long-term trend intact, but the stock needs to weather Fed-driven selling pressure in the near term.
The $11.2B backlog and 65.5% growth acceleration support long-term fundamentals, but macro headwinds and valuation concerns coexist in the near term.

investmentVerdict.summary: Fundamentals are at all-time best with NYT coverage, Wedbush $230 + UBS $200 upgrades, $11.2B backlog, and 65.5% growth acceleration. While Fed hawkishness and a 221x P/E will amplify near-term volatility, the track record of recovering from every 20%+ drawdown and the defense AI moat make buying on dips viable.

threads.text:
NYT highlighted Palantir as the prime example of 'Silicon Valley betting on war and being rewarded.'

Price $152.77, consensus target $194.05 (+27%)
Wedbush $230 (+48%), UBS $200 (+31%) simultaneous upgrades
16 out of 28 analysts rate Buy

ShipOS $448M extends AI to submarine production, with $11.2B backlog and FY2026 revenue accelerating +65.5%. Fundamentals are at all-time best.

However, P/E of 221x in a Fed hawkish environment, UK NHS privacy controversy, and CEO Karp's daily $6M selling are coexisting risks. Below the 50-day MA with RSI at 46, there's near-term correction pressure, but given the track record of recovering from all 10 drawdowns of 20%+ over 3 years, buying dips in tranches remains a valid strategy.

Price $152.77, consensus target $194.05 (+27%)

16 out of 28 analysts rate Buy

ShipOS $448M applies AI to submarine production. $11.2B backlog, FY2026E revenue $7.41B (+65.5%). FCF margin 47%, best-in-class.

Risks: P/E 221x in a Fed hawkish environment. UK NHS privacy controversy. CEO Karp selling $6M daily. Below 50-day MA, RSI 46 neutral.

Recovered from all 10 drawdowns of 20%+ over 3 years. Buying dips in tranches remains valid.

witty.hook: Summary of NYT's unofficial PLTR ad
witty.text:
NYT wrote 'Silicon Valley bet on war, being rewarded.' Palantir shareholders already got their 1,860% reward over 3 years.

Price $152.77, target $194.05 (+27%)
Wedbush sees $230 (+48%) and UBS raised to $200 (+31%).

When a company puts AI in submarines with a $448M contract and has $11.2B in backlog, calling a 221x P/E expensive is a fair point but also kind of beside the point.

Of course CEO Karp is selling $6M daily, the UK is angry about NHS data privacy, and the Fed is in hawk mode. But this stock has recovered from 10 drawdowns of 20%+. Price $152.77, target $194.05 (+27%). Scared? Your call.

empathy.text:
Fed hawkish comments sent the Dow -400. Not an easy day for growth stock holders.

PLTR at $152.77, below the 50-day MA ($158). RSI 46 neutral, but it probably feels worse than that.

Still, NYT highlighted Palantir as a defense AI success story today. Wedbush $230 (+48%), UBS $200 (+31%) simultaneous upgrades. Consensus target $194.05 (+27%).

The UK NHS privacy controversy and CEO selling are concerns, but $11.2B backlog and FY2026 revenue accelerating +65.5% support the fundamentals. The stock has endured 10 drawdowns of 20%+ over 3 years and recovered every single time.

Price $152.77, target $194.05 (+27%). Buy on dips in tranches. Those who hold on ultimately win.
